info.title: AIOps Runner Gateway API
info.description: Internal TLS 1.3/mTLS protocol. Runner identity is derived only from the verified client certificate and server-side registration. All responses are no-store.

get.summary: Return the currently authenticated server-side Runner identity

post.description: This WRITE-only queue operation is the only job operation that returns a raw job lease token. READ always returns 204 and must use /runner/v1/read-tasks/{task_id}:claim instead.

post.summary: Advance one durable credential authorization or anchor phase
post.description: Gateway never holds a Vault manager/revoker token, child token, or dynamic Secret. The closed phases are AUTHORIZE_CHILD_CREATE, RECORD_ANCHOR, ACTIVATE, NO_CREDENTIAL and REQUEST_REVOCATION. Runner cannot submit issuer routes, roles, paths, scopes, TTLs or arbitrary parameters. RECORD_ANCHOR is the only phase carrying a revoke-only accessor, and dynamic issuance is forbidden until its durable ACK.

post.summary: Perform final server-side authorization and enter RUNNING
post.description: The server revalidates signature, approval/CEL policy, environment and target revisions, Safety/Kill Switch, production classification, certificate, registration, scope revision, epoch, and lease token.

Lease extension is selected by the Gateway. Replayed sequences do not extend a lease. Certificate revocation or Runner disable returns 403 without extension; scope revision change or cancellation returns 200 TERMINATE without extension.

post.summary: Release a LEASED job before start
post.description: Only LEASED jobs may be returned to QUEUED. The server derives the detail hash and retry delay from the allowlisted reason code.

post.summary: Persist a certificate-bound result receipt and request credential revocation
post.description: Runner never submits a result hash. Gateway binds job, plan, Runner, client certificate, epoch, scope revision and structured result before JCS/SHA-256.

post.summary: Lease one credential revocation to an authorized WRITE Runner
post.description: This is the only revocation operation that returns a raw claim token and revoke-only accessor.
Runner submits only REVOKED or FAILED plus an allowlisted failure code. Gateway owns retry delay, jitter, exhaustion and manual escalation.

post.summary: Claim the specified persisted READ investigation task
post.description: The task ID is supplied by a Temporal READ activity. This is the only READ task operation that returns a raw lease token; an active claim is never replayed.

post.summary: Reauthorize and start a claimed READ investigation task
post.description: The Gateway authenticates mTLS identity and authorizes the persisted connector, operation and typed input in the same transaction as the state transition.

post.summary: Advance a READ task heartbeat sequence
post.description: Only the next sequence extends the server-selected lease. Scope or parent lifecycle drift yields TERMINATE without extension.

post.summary: Release a claimed READ task before it starts
post.description: Release is permitted only while the persisted attempt remains LEASED and accepts only a closed low-cardinality reason code.

post.summary: Persist a bounded structured READ task result
post.description: The Gateway validates a closed outcome union, applies the trusted connector output contract and atomically persists Evidence, task state, attempt and receipt. Runner-supplied hashes and source metadata are forbidden.

mutualTLS.description: TLS 1.3 client certificate with one canonical Runner SPIFFE URI SAN

jobLease.name: Authorization
jobLease.description: Authorization: AIOPS-Job-Lease <opaque-token>. Values are always redacted from logs and traces.

revocationLease.name: Authorization
revocationLease.description: Authorization: AIOPS-Revocation-Lease <opaque-token>. Values are always redacted from logs and traces.

readTaskLease.name: Authorization
readTaskLease.description: Authorization: AIOPS-Read-Task-Lease <43-character-base64url-token>. The value is accepted only for the matching READ task path and is always redacted from logs and traces.

ReadTaskID.name: task_id
ReadTaskID.description: Lowercase persistent UUID. Invalid or out-of-scope values are masked as not found.

JobLeaseOK.description: One job and its one-time-returned raw lease token

JobStartOK.description: Job entered RUNNING after final authorization

CredentialAnchorOK.description: Durable credential phase authorization or state ACK; never a child token or dynamic Secret

JobCompletionAccepted.description: New result receipt persisted in FINALIZING

RevocationLeaseOK.description: One revocation claim with raw claim token and revoke-only accessor

ReadTaskClaimOK.description: Specified READ task and its one-time-returned raw lease token

ReadTaskStartOK.description: READ task entered RUNNING after transactional final authorization

ReadTaskReleaseOK.description: READ task lease released before start

ReadTaskCompletionOK.description: Atomic READ result receipt persisted or exactly replayed

ReadTaskLeaseAuthenticationFailed.description: Missing or invalid READ task lease Authorization

Problem409.description: Fence, sequence, state, anchor, or result conflict

ReadTaskStateConflict.description: READ task fence or state conflict

ReadTaskHeartbeatConflict.description: READ task fence, state, or heartbeat sequence conflict

ReadTaskResultConflict.description: READ task fence, state, or durable result conflict

Problem422.description: Structured READ result rejected by the registered connector contract

Problem413.description: Request body exceeds its endpoint limit

Problem503.description: Claims are disabled or a required dependency is unavailable

ReadTaskClaimsUnavailable.description: READ task claims are disabled or a required dependency is unavailable

ReadTaskDependencyUnavailable.description: A required READ task dependency is unavailable

ReadTaskPlanBinding.description: Secret-safe immutable investigation plan identity selected and persisted by the server.

ReadTaskRuntimeBinding.description: Secret-safe immutable READ execution identity; digests bind trusted connector, target, executor and aggregate runtime manifests without exposing endpoints or credentials.

input.description: Typed connector input selected from the trusted server registry; arbitrary network targets, headers, credentials and commands are forbidden by that registered schema.

input_hash.description: SHA-256 of the exact JCS-canonical input bytes returned in this descriptor.

items.description: One connector-specific structured item. The trusted server registry performs the final typed validation and redaction.
